data.title: Coinbase Faces Lawsuit Over Bankruptcy Risks & Trading Violations
data.description:
Coinbase, a prominent cryptocurrency exchange platform, is currently facing a legal challenge as it has been sued for allegedly exposing its users to significant bankruptcy risks and for purportedly engaging in various trading violations that may have compromised the integrity of its trading practices. This lawsuit could have serious implications for the company's operations and its reputation...
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/ampstories/web-stories/coinbase-faces-lawsuit-over-bankruptcy-risks-trading-violations-2

data.title: U.S. Senate Committee To Hold Hearing On Bipartisan Digital Asset Legislation
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Senate Banking Subcommittee on Digital Assets is scheduled to hold its inaugural hearing next Wednesday. The session will focus on discussing a bipartisan legislative framework for digital assets.

data.title: U.S. Senate Banking and Digital Assets Subcommittee to hold first hearing on Wednesday
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Taylor Barr posted on the X platform that the U.S. Senate Banking Digital Assets Subcommittee will hold its first hearing on Wednesday to discuss the theme of "Exploring a Bipartisan Legislative Framework for Digital Assets."

data.title: Venture Capitalists Cautious About Investing In AI Agents
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, venture capital firms are showing enthusiasm for artificial intelligence (AI) agents but have yet to make investments in this area. This sentiment was shared during a panel discussion at the Consensus 2025 event in Hong Kong, featuring insights from Paul Veradittakit of Pantera Capital, Dragonfly partner GM, Akshat Vaidya from Maelstrom, and Martin Baumann of CMCC Global.

The panelists discussed the potential of AI agents to revolutionize various aspects of the digital asset space, including high-frequency trading for retail investors and enhancing crypto security. They highlighted the transformative potential of AI agents in becoming a dominant form of user interaction. However, despite these promising prospects, the panelists acknowledged that they have not yet committed capital to AI agents, indicating that it might be premature for such investments.

Veradittakit pointed out significant opportunities for integrating AI agents into the crypto sector, particularly in trading. He suggested that AI-powered trading could exponentially increase trading volumes due to the agents' ability to act swiftly and intelligently on behalf of users. These agents could facilitate seamless entry and exit from various products and execute decentralized finance (DeFi) trades, potentially reshaping the future of financial services.

Dragonfly partner GM emphasized the mutual benefits between AI agents and crypto, noting that crypto could enhance the safety of AI agents. He proposed that AI agents could participate in hackathons with crypto prizes, incentivizing safety improvements. GM described this as an intriguing intersection where crypto aids AI agent research and development.

Despite outlining these potential interactions, the panelists admitted they have not yet invested in AI agents. Vaidya expressed optimism about AI agents' future role in real-world interactions, particularly in regulatory compliance. He envisioned a future where AI agents could execute complex strategies, such as forming LLCs and hiring personnel, fundamentally altering the business landscape.

Baumann mentioned that while CMCC Global is closely monitoring AI agents, their current focus remains on infrastructure. GM echoed this cautious approach, highlighting the challenges of predicting the future landscape, especially for venture capitalists. He stressed the importance of considering long-term developments before making investment decisions.

In conclusion, while AI agents present exciting possibilities, Veradittakit noted that it will take time for these technologies to mature and become viable investment opportunities.

data.title: The number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending February 15 was 219,000, while the expected number was 215,000.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The number of initial jobless claims in the United States for the week ending February 15 was 219,000, which was expected to be 215,000. The previous value was revised from 213,000 to 214,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: FOX reporter: The US SEC is very interested in pledge
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that according to a source who recently spoke with the US SEC, the agency is very interested in pledges and even asked the industry to provide a memo detailing different types of pledges and their benefits. The source expects that the agency will issue some kind of guidance on pledges in the near future because it is a topic they are passionate about.

data.title: SEC Shows Interest In Staking, Guidance Expected Soon
data.description: According to Odaily, Fox Business journalist Eleanor Terrett reported on the X platform that a source recently in conversation with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) revealed the agency's keen interest in staking. The SEC has requested the industry to provide a memorandum detailing various types of staking and their benefits. The source anticipates that the SEC will soon issue guidance on staking, as it is a topic of significant interest to them.

data.title: Soneium launches 100 million ASTR community incentive campaign
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Sony Layer2 project Soneium posted on the X platform that it will provide 100 million ASTR for community incentives.
The Astar Contribution Score (ACS) event will be launched on Soneium, aiming to promote on-chain adoption, expand the role of ASTR in Superchain, and reward ecosystem participation.
By interacting with dApps, participating in Web3 experiences, and contributing to network activities, users will earn ACS points, which will be redeemable for ASTR at the end of the event.
